item.name: PAY-2452 ACH Refunds — QA Test Collection
item.description:
Test collection for ACH Refund epic (PAY-2452).

## Setup
1. Run `postman/seed-refund-test-data.sql` against your Payments database to create test transactions
2. Set `payments-management-api` variable to your environment (e.g., `https://localhost:5001`)
3. Set `access-token` in your Postman environment (inherited via Authorization header)
4. Collection variables are pre-populated with seed script transaction IDs

## Workflow Order
Run folders top-to-bottom:
1. **Prereq** — verify test transaction exists
2. **Happy Path** — full and partial refunds
3. **Eligibility Validation** — all error scenarios
4. **Idempotency** — duplicate handling
5. **Status Verification** — query refund status and history
6. **Blocking Rule** — RETURNED child blocks further refunds

## Key Changes in v2 Epic
- Partial refund → original status PARTIALLY_REFUNDED (18)
- Full refund → original status REFUNDED (10)
- Refund credit return → refund txn RETURNED (7), original conditional
- Blocking rule: any child RETURNED blocks ALL future refunds
- Event name: `Payment.Refund.Submitted` (singular)
- Refundable amount calc: only APPROVED, FUNDED, SETTLED count

item.name: PAY-3605 Unmatched Settlement Alert (Treasury)
item.description:
E2E driver for the Unmatched Settlement Transaction Alert feature (treasury-ms).

The alert has NO UI and NO dedicated endpoint — it fires internally during a funding-batch run. This collection drives the only HTTP lever: POST /create-funding-batch.

USAGE
1. Import this collection AND an environment file (see PAY-3605-*.postman_environment.json). Select the DEV environment.
2. Fill `clientSecret` in the environment (from Key Vault) — do NOT commit it.
3. The collection-level pre-request script auto-mints a client_credentials token and caches it until ~60s before expiry, so you can just hit '2. Trigger' repeatedly across Scenarios A/B/C/D.
4. Verify in App Insights (KQL) + SendGrid Activity Feed + inbox — telemetry alone does NOT prove delivery.

The runbook (PAY-3605-Story-1.1-E2E-Unmatched-Settlement-Alert-Runbook.md) is the source of truth for scenarios, seed SQL, and pass criteria.

Collection vars: identityUrl, treasuryHost, clientId, scope

request.description: Optional. The collection pre-request script already mints a token automatically. Use this only to debug auth (e.g. confirm the returned `scope` includes WellfitPaymentsAPI.DynamicFunding). A 401/invalid_client here means wrong clientId/secret; a token without the scope means the client isn't granted DynamicFunding (check [Identity].[ClientScopes]).

exec: if (pm.response.code === 503) { console.warn('503 — coordinator queue full. Re-send.'); }
if (pm.response.code === 401) { console.error('401 — token missing/!DynamicFunding. Run "1. Get Token" to inspect.'); }
try { const j = pm.response.json(); console.log('correlationId =', j.correlationId, '| stage =', j.stage); } catch (e) {}
console.log('Now wait ~15-60s, then run the App Insights verify query. 202 != done — the orchestrator runs on the background coordinator.');

request.description:
Fires a funding-batch run. Returns 202 immediately with { correlationId, stage }. The unmatched-settlement orchestrator runs seconds later on the coordinator.

Per scenario (seed the DB first per the runbook Appendix A):
- A: 4 seeded rows  -> one email + UnmatchedAlertSent
- B: empty table    -> no email + UnmatchedAlertSuppressed
- C: re-seed, send TWICE without deleting -> two emails (re-alert-until-cleared, D2)
- D: kill-switch OFF (+restart) -> no email, no telemetry at all

503 = queue full (retry). 401 = token problem.

url.raw: https://api.applicationinsights.io/v1/apps/{{appInsightsAppId}}/query?query=customEvents | where timestamp > ago(30m) | where name in ('UnmatchedAlertSent','UnmatchedAlertSuppressed','UnmatchedAlertSendFailed') | project timestamp, name, customDimensions | order by timestamp desc

- aiBearer:          az account get-access-token --resource https://api.applicationinsights.io --query accessToken -o tsv

item.name: PAY-3627 STAGE — ACH Limit -> Worldpay MAX (PUT-driven, no synthetic publish)
item.description:
STAGE QA collection for PAY-3627. Unlike the DEV collection, this NEVER publishes a synthetic CloudEvent to Event Grid (STAGE QA does not have EventGrid Data Sender and we are not opening that up). Instead every AC is driven through the real SubMerchant API write endpoint:

    PUT {{SUBMERCHANT_API}}/api/submerchants/{id}/ach-limits   (policy: ach-limits-admin)

The handler (UpdateAchLimitConfigHandler) upserts AchLimitConfig + audit log, then publishes the real SubMerchant.AchLimitConfig.Updated event. The publisher itself computes prev/new Worldpay MAX = MAX(card limit, ACH limit) and the maxTransactionAmountChanged hint, so QA never hand-crafts the event payload. merchant-provisioning-ms consumes the event and pushes MAX to Vantiv.

STAGE Identity (verified 2026-06-22): scope WellfitSubMerchantAPI.AchLimitsAdmin EXISTS and is granted to client WellfitUnifiedPaymentsAPI. IMPORTANT: that client holds AchLimitsAdmin but NOT WellfitSubMerchantAPI.Read — so (a) request ONLY AchLimitsAdmin in the token (adding .Read makes the client_credentials call fail with invalid_scope), and (b) you cannot GET the SubMerchant with this client. The card limit is therefore derived from BASELINE_MAX on a FRESH SubMerchant (BASELINE_MAX == card when no ACH config exists). Use a fresh SubMerchant.

Why the AC order differs from DEV: on the PUT path you induce maxTransactionAmountChanged via DATA (the ACH value relative to the card limit), not by setting a flag. So the short-circuit ACs (AC2/AC3) must run from a clean baseline (ACH <= card) BEFORE AC1 raises the MAX. Session order: 00 -> 01 -> 02 -> 03(AC3) -> 04(AC2) -> 05(AC1) -> 06(Verify) -> 07(AC9) -> 08(AC4) -> 99(Cleanup).

Set manually before running (see PAY-3627-STAGE-Environment):
  - SM_CLIENT_SECRET — STAGE secret for WellfitUnifiedPaymentsAPI (per-env, from Key Vault — NOT Test123!)
  - MP_CLIENT_SECRET — for the Vantiv ground-truth read
  - PV2_CLIENT_SECRET — for AC9 payment submission (WellfitUnifiedPaymentsAPI also covers PV2)
  - SUB_MERCHANT_ID — a fresh, fully-provisioned STAGE SubMerchant

Source of truth: PAY-3627-STAGE-PUT-Path-Runbook-Addendum.md (same folder).

The client must hold WellfitSubMerchantAPI.AchLimitsAdmin and you must request ONLY that scope (SM_SCOPE). Do NOT add .Read — WellfitUnifiedPaymentsAPI does not have it and the request will fail.').to.be.a('string'));
